notes: Nicola's keeper-loop verbs. PLANT / TEND / HARVEST / SET THE TABLE as starters. Buyable pool is GARDEN cards — recurring crops + cuttings that bear over multiple turns. Bloom is the second stat (rises GOOD, opposite of Stagnation). Doubt still rises bad. HARVEST is the spendable currency.

cards.title: PLANT
cards.flavor: Put a seed in the clay pot — basil from the jar that has been the basil jar since '08. The boat keeps the soil warm even in February. Bloom rises.

cards.title: TEND
cards.flavor: Water, prune, talk to the rosebush — the one Nicola's mother gave her in '99. The rosebush listens. Bloom rises; doubt softens by the amount it softens when something growing is told it's growing well.

cards.title: HARVEST
cards.flavor: Pick what's ready — by feel, the way Nicola does it without looking. Carry it down through the hatch. The pantry shelf takes another jar in its own handwriting.

cards.title: SET THE TABLE
cards.flavor: Lay places — twelve plates on the long table, the dishes Nicola's mother gave her, the ones with the small chip in the rim of plate seven. The guests come to where the plates are. They always have.

cards.title: SERVE
cards.flavor: Bring a dish to a guest at the table. Costs one harvest from the pantry.

cards.title: POUR
cards.flavor: Pour wine, water, coffee — whatever they came for. Loosens a guest who's having trouble landing.

cards.title: THE TIDELINE
cards.flavor: Mark a guest's place at the table. The next time you bring them anything, both of you are also bringing each other a memory neither of you knew you still had.

cards.title: GARDEN CUTTING
cards.flavor: Take a cutting from a plant. The cutting bears next turn.

cards.title: PANTRY PULL
cards.flavor: Open a jar dated three seasons ago — the September tomato preserve, in Nicola's hand on a hand-cut label. The room is briefly that season. The whole boat smells like late summer for two minutes.

cards.title: THE RADIO
cards.flavor: Turn the wheelhouse radio on. The static is friendly — the kind that has been friendly to her since the boat was Bill's. A nearby boat answers; the rosebush hears it through the porthole.
